differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Server-Side Rendering is a core feature for initial load and SEO optimization.
differences.rightDesc: Handles requests efficiently, leveraging web standards for routing and data loading.

differences.title: 2. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Explicit data fetching methods tied to page generation or server-side execution.
differences.rightDesc: Uses `loader` functions colocated with routes for declarative data fetching.

differences.title: 3. ROUTING
differences.leftDesc: Pages are created by exporting React components from files in the `pages` directory.
differences.rightDesc: Routes are defined by file structure, with special files for layout and actions.

differences.title: 4. FORMS HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Requires manual state management or external libraries for form submissions.
differences.rightDesc: Provides `useFetcher` and `useMutation` hooks for seamless form submissions.

differences.title: 5. CSS SUPPORT
differences.leftDesc: Supports various CSS solutions like CSS Modules, Tailwind CSS, and styled-components.
differences.rightDesc: Emphasizes global CSS and native browser features for styling.

differences.title: 6. API ROUTES
differences.leftDesc: Create API endpoints within the `pages/api` directory for backend logic.
differences.rightDesc: Leverages `route.js` files in app directory for serverless API endpoints.

differences.title: 7. DEPLOYMENT
differences.leftDesc: Highly integrated with Vercel for optimized static and serverless deployments.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for the edge, works well with various hosting platforms.
